result:
Fleshed petition written to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/3/patron-baseline/writs-page-width.md`.

The brief was a UI layout complaint: writs page goes edge-to-edge and feels cluttered; should match Spider/Guild and get card treatment. I expanded it into:

- **Reader/decision**: an operator (patron or anima steward) scanning writs many times a day to triage what's in flight and what's stuck.
- **Scope**: adopt the shared page-shell, wrap header/filters/list regions in cards, preserve all existing functionality. Explicitly out: table redesign, other pages' layouts, responsive rework, new filters.
- **How it works**: reuse whatever page-shell Spider/Guild use (extract if needed), two or three cards (header, filters, list) using existing design tokens, success measured by flipping between the three pages feeling identical in outer rhythm.
- **Assumptions/deferred questions**: flagged that Spider and Guild may not actually be aligned, asked which is canonical, asked about internal-scroll behavior, and confirmed this is layout-only.
